name: World-Class Chef-Inspired Avocado Toast
description: Elevated avocado toast featuring creamy textured avocado mash on thick sourdough, topped with precision-cooked 63°C egg, confit cherry tomatoes, toasted pine nuts, black sesame, and microgreens. A luxurious brunch dish inspired by fine-dining techniques.
keywords: avocado toast, sourdough, sous vide egg, gourmet breakfast, chef recipe
chef: Inspired by Gordon Ramsay & Bobby Flay techniques

ingredients.item: red pepper flakes or Aleppo pepper
toasted pine nuts or sliced almonds

ingredients.note: mixed with lemon zest and sea salt

steps.instruction: Prepare thick, even slices of sourdough using a precision bread knife. Toast until deeply golden and crisp on the outside, chewy inside. Optionally torch edges lightly for char.

Make the avocado base: In a mortar and pestle, gently crush avocado flesh with minced shallot, lime juice, lemon zest, chopped herbs, olive oil, chili flakes, salt, and pepper to a creamy yet chunky texture.
Cook eggs using sous-vide immersion circulator at 63–65°C for 45–60 minutes for silky custardy yolks. Keep warm.
Prepare confit cherry tomatoes by slow-cooking in olive oil with garlic and thyme at low temperature, or lightly torch fresh ones.

Assemble: Drizzle toasted bread with olive oil and flaky salt. Spread avocado mixture evenly. Top with egg, confit tomatoes, toasted nuts, black sesame blend, microgreens, and a final drizzle of olive oil or aged balsamic.
Serve immediately while toast is warm and crisp.

winePairings.name: Crisp Sauvignon Blanc
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and citrus notes cut through the richness of the avocado and complement the lime and herbs perfectly.

winePairings.name: Chablis or unoaked Chardonnay
winePairings.notes: Mineral-driven white with subtle oak to match the toasted sourdough and nutty elements.

steps.instruction: Combine ingredients in a small saucepan and cook over very low heat (around 200°F/93°C) for 20-30 minutes until tomatoes are softened but hold shape.
Cool slightly and use as topping. Store leftover oil for other uses.
